documentation.value: Receives any number of arguments and prints their values to stdout, separated by tabs, with a newline at the end.
Values to print. Each is converted using tostring().
Returns the type of its argument as a string: "nil", "number", "string", "boolean", "table", "function", "thread", or "userdata".
The value to check the type of.
Converts any value to a string in a human-readable format. Uses the __tostring metamethod if available.
The value to convert to a string.
Converts a value to a number. Returns nil if conversion is not possible. An optional base (2-36) can be specified for string conversion.
The value to convert.
Optional numeric base for string conversion (2-36). Default is 10.
Returns a formatted string following the description given in formatstring, similar to C's sprintf.
A format string with specifiers like %s, %d, %f, %q, etc.
Values to be formatted.

signatures.label: string.find(s, pattern, init?, plain?): number, number | nil

documentation.value: Looks for the first match of pattern in string s. Returns start and end indices, or nil if not found.
The string to search in.
The Lua pattern to search for.
Position to start the search. Defaults to 1.
If true, turns off pattern matching and does a plain find.

signatures.label: string.gsub(s, pattern, repl, n?): string, number

documentation.value: Returns a copy of s in which all (or the first n) occurrences of the pattern have been replaced. Also returns the total number of matches.
The source string.
The Lua pattern to match.
Replacement string, table, or function.
Maximum number of replacements. Defaults to all.
Inserts element value at position pos in list, shifting up other elements. If pos is omitted, appends to the end.
The table (used as an array) to insert into.
Optional position index. Defaults to end of list.
The value to insert.
Removes from list the element at position pos, shifting down other elements. Returns the removed value. If pos is omitted, removes the last element.
The table (used as an array) to remove from.
The position of the element to remove. Defaults to the last element.
Sorts list elements in place. If comp is given, it must be a function that receives two elements and returns true when the first should come before the second.
The table (used as an array) to sort.
Optional comparison function (a, b) -> boolean.
Returns the argument with the maximum value according to the Lua operator <.
First number.
Additional numbers to compare.
Returns the argument with the minimum value according to the Lua operator <.
First number.
Additional numbers to compare.
Opens a file in the specified mode. Returns a file handle on success, or nil and an error message on failure.
The path to the file to open.
Open mode: 'r' (read), 'w' (write), 'a' (append), 'r+', 'w+', 'a+'. Append 'b' for binary.
Reads from the default input file according to the given formats. Common formats: '*l' (line), '*n' (number), '*a' (all).
Read format strings: '*l' (line), '*n' (number), '*a' (entire file), or a number for byte count.
Calls function f with the given arguments in protected mode. Returns true plus results on success, or false plus an error message on failure.
The function to call in protected mode.
Arguments to pass to the function.
